name: Rufus Jones
abstract: Rufus "Speedy" Jones (May 27, 1936 – April 25, 1990) was an American jazz drummer from Charleston, South Carolina. Starting out on trumpet, Jones switched to drums at the age of 13.

works.t: Things Ain't What They Used To Be
Second Sacred Concert: The Shepherd (Who Watches Over the Night Flock)

about_quotes.speaker: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Barney Bigard) does not mention Rufus Jones; the interviewer mentions Rufus Jones in relation to Duke Ellington ('I saw that in operation with Rufus Jones...').

about_quotes.speaker: Wallace Davenport
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Rufus Jones. The drummer. Yeah. [INTERVIEWEE] Rufus Jones on drums. Yeah. Rufus Jones on drums.

about_quotes.speaker: John Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: Then there was Rufus Jones, and then there was the other Jones, Elvin Jones was there for a short time.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: And I am playing it, I‟m walking you know the tempo that Rufus gave me. And Rufus starts playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Richmond, Virginia, Jackie Byard and Rufus Jones were on the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Rufus Jones (PHONETIC) came into the band, out of the Ellington orchestra.
